name: Blade Fighter
effect: +1 base die per talent level to close combat rolls when fighting with blade weapons

name: Bodyguard
description: Diving in to protect allies from attacks
effect: If someone within Short range is hit by an attack, roll for Agility to take the hit instead. Can push the roll.

name: Bowman
effect: +1 base die per talent level when firing a bow or crossbow

name: Defender
effect: +1 base die per talent level when blocking close combat attacks

name: Demolitions Expert
effect: +1 base die per talent level when using explosive weapons, including hand grenades and improvised explosives

name: Dirty Fighter
effect: Your unarmed close combat attacks have a base damage rating of 2

name: Evasive
effect: +1 base die per talent level to Agility rolls for dodging ranged attacks

name: Executioner
effect: Roll an extra die when inflicting a critical hit on a target and choose the result you prefer

name: Fast Reflexes
effect: Draw an additional initiative card at the start of combat; choose the card you want to use

name: Heavy Weapons
effect: +1 base die per talent level when firing heavy weapons such as rocket launchers, flamers, and vehicle mounted weapons

name: Pistoleer
effect: +1 base die per talent level when firing a pistol

name: Polearms
effect: +1 base die per talent level to close combat rolls when using blunt weapons

name: Pugilist
effect: +1 base die per talent level to close combat rolls when fighting unarmed

name: Sharpshooter
effect: +1 base die per talent level when firing a long barreled gun, such as a rifle or a carbine

name: Actor
effect: +1 base die per talent level when rolling to bluff or tell a lie to an NPC

name: Agitator
effect: +1 base die per talent level when rolling for Empathy to speak to a group of NPCs to sway or convince them

name: Barter
effect: +1 base die per talent level when rolling for Empathy to trade or buy something

name: Charmer
effect: +1 base die per talent level when rolling for Empathy to make an NPC like you

name: Cook
effect: +1 base die per talent level when cooking using a field kitchen

name: Interrogator
effect: +1 base die per talent level to rolls for extracting information from someone

name: Malicious
effect: Inflict one point of despair for each six when threatening an NPC

name: Mentalist
effect: +1 base die per talent level to Empathy rolls for reading a subject and understanding if they are lying or hiding something

name: Musician
effect: +1 base die per talent level to Empathy when using a musical instrument

name: Renowned
effect: You can push any roll based on Empathy twice, not just once

name: Thug
effect: Roll for Strength instead of Empathy when threatening someone, +1 base die per talent level

name: Driver
effect: +1 base die per talent level for driving any kind of ground vehicle

name: Exo-Specialist
effect: +1 base die per talent level when handling an exo of some kind

name: Greatship Pilot
effect: +1 base die per talent level to rolls for piloting large spaceships

name: Kite Operator
effect: +1 base die per talent level to rolls when controlling a kite

name: Shuttle Pilot
effect: +1 base die per talent level to Agility rolls for piloting a shuttle

name: Archaeology
effect: +1 base die per talent level for understanding ruins and monuments

name: Artifact Specialist
effect: +1 base die per talent level to comprehend artifacts

name: Astrometry
effect: +1 base die per talent level to understand space phenomena, orbital mechanics, and planetology

name: Botanist
effect: +1 base die per talent level for cultivating plants or fungi as well as understanding flora and ivy

name: Cartographer
effect: +1 base die per talent level when mapping and navigating during treks

name: Cultural Savant
effect: +1 base die per talent level to understand customs and cultural habits in the Lost Horizon

name: Data Djinn
effect: +1 base die per talent level when interacting with computers and terminals

name: Glyph Scholar
effect: +1 base die per talent level to comprehend glyphs or decrypt old texts

name: Historian
effect: +1 base die per talent level to know historical facts about the Diaspora, the Lost Horizon, and the Old Horizon

name: Investigator
effect: +1 base die per talent level when searching an area for clues

name: Laboratorist
effect: +1 base die per talent level for making concoctions and understanding chemical reactions

name: Librarian
effect: +1 base die per talent level when using a library or info cubes to find information

name: Quartermastery
effect: +1 base die per talent level when setting up camp and recovery rolls in camps

name: Ruin Delver
effect: +1 base die per talent level when leading the way through a ruin or vault

name: Teratology
effect: +1 base die per talent level to know and understand the beasts of the Great Dark

name: Bird Handler
effect: +1 base die per talent level for handling a Bird

name: Intuition
effect: Once per session, ask the GM for vague but useful guidance

name: Electro-Specialist
effect: +1 base die per talent level for manipulating and repairing electronic devices, including computers

name: Jury-Rig
effect: +1 base die per talent level when rolling to craft or jury-rig mechanical machinery

name: Mechanic
effect: +1 base die per talent level for repairing vehicles and mechanical devices; also +1 per level to close combat with tools

name: Miner
effect: +1 base die per talent level to drill, dig, or secure an underground cave or tunnel

name: Permit
effect: You have the proper permits to buy restricted items and carry firearms in Ship City

name: Scan Operator
effect: +1 base die per talent level to scan an area for its configuration, Blight, or other threats

name: Commander
effect: +1 base die per talent level when trying to help a person broken by despair

name: Field Surgeon
effect: +1 base die per talent level when trying to stabilize a critical injury

name: Lone Wolf
effect: You can help yourself when broken by despair; roll for Insight, cannot push the roll

name: Medic
effect: +1 base die per talent level when trying to help someone broken by damage

name: Nine Lives
effect: After a D66 roll for a critical injury, choose which die is the tens number and which is the ones number

name: Nurse
effect: If you spend a shift caring for a patient, they heal 2 points of damage instead of 1; care for patients up to your Logic score

name: Survivor
effect: Roll for Strength when getting a lethal critical injury; on success, stabilized after one shift instead of dead

name: Acrobat
effect: +1 base die per talent level to jumping, climbing, and running

name: Assassin
effect: +1 base die per talent level to perform a sneak attack

name: Burglar
effect: +1 base die per talent level for breaking or picking locks

name: Disguise
effect: +1 base die per talent level to avoid being recognized, and to spot a disguise

name: Hunter
effect: +1 base die per talent level for hunting creatures, beasts, and critters

name: Lookout
effect: +1 base die per talent level to rolls for spotting approaching threats

name: Sixth Sense
effect: You cannot be taken by surprise; sneak attackers get no bonus against you, you draw initiative normally when ambushed

name: Sleight of Hand
effect: +1 base die per talent level to rolls for playing tricks with your hands and pickpocketing

name: Stealthy
effect: +1 base die per talent level for staying hidden; cannot be used for ambushes

name: Streetwise
effect: +1 base die per talent level for acquiring stolen goods, finding a contact, or hearing rumors in Ship City and the colonies

name: Tracker
effect: +1 base die per talent level to find and follow tracks through the wilderness or in delves

name: Zero-G Training
effect: You suffer no negative effects when operating in zero gravity environments

name: Blight Resistant
effect: Maximum Heart score increased by 1 per talent level

name: Endurance
effect: +1 base die per talent level for resisting vacuum, suffocation, and cold

name: Force
effect: +1 base die per talent level to lift, push, or break something heavy or solid

name: Hardened
effect: Roll base dice equal to talent level when suffering Blight; for each six rolled, ignore one point of Blight

name: Hopeful
effect: Maximum Hope score increased by 1 per talent level

name: Pack Mule
effect: Carry limit increases by two more items per level in this talent

name: Reckless
effect: You can push any roll based on Agility twice

name: Resilient
effect: +1 base die per talent level for resisting hunger, thirst, and disease

name: Smart
effect: You can push any roll based on Logic twice

name: Stamina
effect: +1 base die per talent level to perform first aid on yourself

name: Tough
effect: Maximum Health score increased by 1 per talent level
